title: eulalia quadrinervis var. hirtifolia
intro: Eulalia quadrinervis var. hirtifolia, also known as the hairy Eulalia, is a type of grass belonging to the Poaceae family, native to the Himalayan region. The hairy Eulalia is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and aiding in digestion. Traditionally, the hairy Eulalia is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
